layers.name: Presentation Layer
layers.overview: The top-most layer handling all user-facing interactions. It seamlessly switches between a windowed desktop environment and an installable mobile PWA depending on the device viewport.

layers.name: Application Layer
layers.overview: A suite of functional, windowed applications that operate within the OS ecosystem, ranging from standard utilities to complex integrations like a Monaco code editor.

layers.name: Core OS Layer
layers.overview: The essential systems that make the web app behave like a real operating system. This handles window lifecycles, focus management, global shortcuts, and the taskbar.

layers.name: Intelligence Layer
layers.overview: The localized 'AI Brain' that provides semantic understanding. It parses user input, determines intent, and executes corresponding system actions without relying on a backend LLM.

layers.name: State Layer
layers.overview: The global data management backbone. Independent modular stores manage different domains of the OS state, ensuring highly decoupled and performant updates.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.overview: The internal knowledge base and mock file system representing the developer's portfolio contents (projects, skills, achievements) in structured formats.

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.overview: The external services, backend API, and deployment platforms that power real-time capabilities and host the application.
